rationale: The citing paper uses Swait, Argo, and Li (2018) as a key prior goal-based choice model in its theoretical positioning, not just as a background mention. It repeatedly contrasts its own model against that work, including a dedicated comparison table and explicit discussion of what Swait et al. do (e.g., allowing attributes to affect multiple goals) versus what they do not do (e.g., mediation testing and process heterogeneity in attribute-to-goal effects). These contrasts motivate the new model's core design choices and claimed contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper refines the prior goal-based modeling stream represented by Swait et al. (2018) by introducing an explicit multiple-mediation structure with direct and indirect attribute effects and richer process heterogeneity. It positions its approach as an alternative that addresses limitations it attributes to that earlier model class.
